title: The Cavalry of Rome
designIntent: DSK-composed WebGPU/WebGL-first fidelity route with no in-game UI DOM or loading overlay UI: scan over a larger pannable realistic Roman terrain map, enter a Rome-perspective WebGL2-shaded hex battlefield, and use seven scene-native maneuvers with action points, dice rolls, movement highlights, terrain movement rules, and mini low-poly squad units.
standingIterationRule: Every future gameplay iteration should improve visual fidelity as a secondary goal and should not regress terrain density, vegetation descriptors, lighting mood, region readability, soldier fidelity, hex readability, maneuver feedback, or cinematic composition.

currentGameplayStep.intent: Add the first playable tactical rules layer without reintroducing UI: action points, maneuver selection, dice rolls, movement highlights, terrain restrictions, and Rome-only unit control.
currentGameplayStep.implementation: hex-gameplay-pass.js adds Advance Left/Center/Right, Line Brigade, Heavy Brigade, Berserk, and Scout. It rolls 2d6 action points every three turns, uses crypto-backed d6 rolls for advance unit counts, renders shaded WebGL dice on the board, highlights eligible units and reachable hexes, blocks water, ends the maneuver after landing on hills/fences, allows light troops to move up to two spaces, allows Scout to move three spaces, allows Berserk to move two spaces then attack an adjacent enemy, and exposes GameHost tactical gameplay snapshots. It also supersedes older high-noon unit shadows with individual angled soldier shadows.

advanceLeft.effect: Roll 1d6 and move that many eligible Rome units from the left third.

advanceCenter.effect: Roll 1d6 and move that many eligible Rome units from the center third.

advanceRight.effect: Roll 1d6 and move that many eligible Rome units from the right third.

lineBrigade.effect: Move a connected adjacent group of Rome units.

berserk.effect: Move one Rome unit up to two spaces and attack an adjacent enemy.

scout.effect: Move one Rome unit up to three spaces.

fidelityFocus.terrain: realistic low-poly terrain with domain warping, FBM/ridged noise, blended biome colors, moisture/slope/river masks, and non-repeating landforms
fidelityFocus.mapNavigation: large pannable region-selection surface with keyboard/drag pan and wheel zoom
fidelityFocus.regions: oversized highlighted region meshes with glow, wobble borders, and DSK affordance descriptors

hexBattlefield.camera: above and behind Rome, looking across the whole tactical field
hexBattlefield.grid: 11x9 fixed pointy-offset hex board with consistent radius/y-scale covering the old freeform battlefield
hexBattlefield.renderer: WebGL2 shader pass for tile interiors plus Canvas2D overlays for units, movement highlights, and tactical effects
hexBattlefield.materials: procedural FBM terrain color, rim lighting, bevel shading, water shimmer, hill contouring, grass striations, and fence rail/post detail
hexBattlefield.terrain: grass, water, hill, and fence hexes with movement/defense metadata
hexBattlefield.units: mini low-poly soldier squads representing aggregated troop groups, with no circular bases or unit rings
hexBattlefield.selection: only Rome-side units are hover-highlighted and selectable
hexBattlefield.movement: scene-native reachable-hex highlights, water impassable, hill/fence landing ends the maneuver
hexBattlefield.dice: WebGL2 shaded dice for 1d6 advance rolls and 2d6 action point rolls
hexBattlefield.colors: light troops green, medium troops blue, heavy troops red; army ownership appears as a colored military band
hexBattlefield.enemyBands: region-based gold, white, purple, or black bands

fidelityFocus.uiDom: none; runtime status hooks are non-DOM sinks only
fidelityFocus.loaderUi: none; shared route page loader is intentionally omitted for this game endpoint

proceduralVegetation.grass: deterministic grass descriptor field is retained as metadata for future terrain-anchored instancing
proceduralVegetation.screenSpaceRendering: disabled because it was visually floating in the sky and caused panning/selection jank
proceduralVegetation.battlefieldGrounding: tile features draw directly on the hex playfield so they cannot detach from terrain
proceduralVegetation.boundary: local procedural vegetation descriptor DSK candidate owns placements; no screen-space overlay is rendered until true terrain-anchored WebGPU instancing exists

fidelityFocus.soldiers: tactical hex mode uses mini low-poly soldier squads with individual angled shadows, body class colors, military bands, shields, spears, helmets, and selection pennants
fidelityFocus.battlefield: aligned WebGL2-shaded hex grid, terrain tiles, scene-native highlights, shaded dice, aggregated squad pieces, region terrain variation, and no visible UI

protectedExpressionGuardrails: Do not copy tabletop scenario layouts, card systems, dice faces, command sections, rule text, or unit-stat tables.
Keep the route visual-first until the cinematic terrain and battlefield proof is stable.
Use Roman-history flavor as original visual composition, not as a digital recreation of a board-game ruleset.
currentLocalSurfaces: realistic terrain mesh color and brush-stroke overlay generation
large pannable region ellipse placement and labels
procedural vegetation descriptor generation without screen-space presentation
Rome-perspective aligned WebGL2 hex battlefield overlay
reachable hex and attack target highlighter
cinematic camera interpolation and map pan/zoom controls
non-DOM runtime sink shim for status/readout compatibility

rendererOwnership.hexGameplayOverlay: scene-native movement/highlight/squad renderer and first playable interaction layer
dskOwned: procedural vegetation descriptor snapshot via local DSK candidate
hex battlefield snapshot via local DSK candidate
tactical gameplay snapshot via local DSK candidate
